layer_model.L1_input_text: Raw invoice text quality/format issues before extraction
layer_model.L2_extraction_proposal: LLM extraction produces malformed or incorrect structured output
layer_model.L3_evidence_binding: Verifier rejects extraction due to grounding/consistency failures
layer_model.L4_ambiguity_conflict: Multiple valid interpretations prevent deterministic resolution
layer_model.L5_routing_unreachable: Upstream gate blocks entry to routing layer

failure_families.name: Table-format arithmetic corruption (synthetic data)
failure_families.description: Synthetic invoices with table-like formatting and multiple candidate totals fail the full extraction pipeline due to raw-text arithmetic inconsistency. The extraction itself succeeds — verify_extraction() passes 12/12 — but check_arithmetic() rejects the document because line-item values do not sum to the stated grand total. This is a document-level data quality issue, not an extraction or evidence-grounding failure.
symptoms: verify_extraction() passes 12/12 — extraction itself is correct at the evidence-grounding level
Invoice has multiple numeric values near total-like keywords (descriptive, not causal)
likely_causes: Synthetic invoice generator produces arithmetically inconsistent documents (line items do not sum to stated grand total)
For the currently evaluated FF-001 cases, arithmetic corruption is the actual blocker; OCR noise appears secondary rather than primary
The original L2 classification was based on surface symptoms (table formatting, multiple totals) without testing whether extraction itself was actually failing
failure_families.evidence_basis: 12 of 17 synthetic_noisy_ocr blocked records carry both table_like and multiple_totals tags. The compound predicate synthetic+noisy_ocr+table_like+multiple_totals achieves 12 TP / 13 flagged = 92.3% precision for predicting EXCEPTION_BAD_EXTRACTION. 1 false positive (INV-2020) carries same tags but passes extraction.
candidate_interventions: Fix synthetic invoice generator to produce arithmetically consistent documents
Evaluate whether ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH handling should differ for known-synthetic test data
Redirect rescue efforts to failure families where extraction quality is actually the bottleneck
failure_families.invariant_note: 92.3% precision / 70.6% recall — 1 FP and 5 FN remain. Not deterministic.
failure_families.priority_rationale: Largest single failure cluster (12/25 = 48% of all blocked), but not addressable by extraction rescue. Root cause is synthetic data quality (arithmetic corruption), not a pipeline limitation. RP-001 prototype confirmed that prompt-level rescue cannot address L1 input-integrity failures.
failure_families.recommended_next_action: Fix synthetic invoice generator; reassess with arithmetically consistent test data
failure_families.notes: All 12 are synthetic with OCR noise injection. Real-world table invoices may present differently and may have correct arithmetic — if so, the extraction pipeline would likely succeed without rescue. RP-001 oracle-assisted prototype falsified the original rescue hypothesis (2026-03-15). See artifacts/RP_001_PROTOTYPE/FINDINGS.md.

prototype_result.finding: Extraction passes verifier 12/12. Sole blocker is ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH (L1 input text). Prompt-level rescue cannot address this.

failure_families.overlap_note: INV-2009 and INV-2030 also had has_po.evidence:null (same fixture issue as FF-002). After fixture patch (2026-03-15), they pass schema validation but still fail arithmetic (ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH). They remain correctly classified as FF-001/L1.

failure_families.name: No-PO email-style OCR — eval fixture integrity (resolved)
failure_families.description: Reclassified: The 5 FF-002 cases were rejected at schema validation because has_po.evidence was null in mock_extractions, not because extraction quality was insufficient. This was an evaluation fixture defect, not a production bottleneck. Fixed by patching null evidence to empty string (2026-03-15). After fix, all 5 cases pass the full validation pipeline and reach EXCEPTION_NO_PO (their expected status).
symptoms: Root cause: has_po.evidence:null fails JSON Schema validation (Step 3 of _validate_extraction_pipeline), before verify_extraction() runs
Post-fix: all 5 cases pass full pipeline and route to EXCEPTION_NO_PO
likely_causes: Fixture data issue: mock_extractions in expected.jsonl had null evidence for has_po field
JSON Schema (extraction_payload_v1.json) requires evidence to be type 'string'; null is not valid in Draft 2020-12
Schema validation gate runs before verify_extraction(), causing early rejection
failure_families.evidence_basis: FF-002 validation-path trace (ff002_trace_20260315T132024Z) confirmed all 5 cases fail at schema validation. RP-002 oracle-assisted prototype confirmed verify_extraction() passes 5/5 and check_arithmetic() passes 5/5 when run directly. Counterfactual (null→empty string) passes full pipeline 5/5 via _validate_extraction_pipeline().
failure_families.priority_rationale: Resolved. eval_fixture_integrity issue. Fixture patched 2026-03-15. All 5 cases now reach EXCEPTION_NO_PO.
failure_families.recommended_next_action: No action needed. Fixture patched. Re-run eval confirmed 5 cases reach EXCEPTION_NO_PO.
failure_families.notes: Production relevance: low — real LLM returns string evidence, not null. Evaluation relevance: high — this issue distorted blocked-rate metrics and failure taxonomy. See artifacts/FF_002_TRACE/FINDINGS.md.

prototype_result.finding: Standard extraction passes verify_extraction() 5/5 and check_arithmetic() 5/5 (or N/A). Bottleneck was schema validation rejecting null evidence in mock_extractions.

trace_result.finding: All 5 cases fail at schema validation (Step 3). has_po.evidence:null violates payload schema. Counterfactual (null→empty string) passes full pipeline 5/5.

failure_families.name: Extended-field extraction failure (invoice_date + tax_amount)
failure_families.description: Invoices requiring extraction of optional fields (invoice_date, tax_amount) fail verification. The verifier's date parsing and tax anchoring logic rejects extractions that cannot meet the stricter evidence requirements for these fields.
symptoms: Invoice expected to have invoice_date and tax_amount in expected_fields
Confirmed failure codes: DATE_AMBIGUOUS (3 cases), TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH (2 cases), ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH (1 case at arithmetic step)
likely_causes: Ambiguous slash-delimited dates where both components ≤ 12 (e.g., 12/03, 03/10, 09/08) — _parse_date_token cannot disambiguate MM/DD vs DD/MM → DATE_AMBIGUOUS (confirmed: INV-1070, INV-1073, INV-1075)

_TAX_ANCHOR_VALUE_RE captures first number after anchor keyword within 24 non-digit chars — when evidence contains embedded percentage (e.g., 'VAT (19%): EUR 699.20'), regex captures '19' instead of '699.20' → TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH (confirmed: INV-1071, INV-1072)
Invoice with discount line fails check_arithmetic (ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH) despite passing verifier — possible L1 overlap (confirmed: INV-1074)
Only 7 gold records test these fields — sample too small for reliable intervention design
failure_families.evidence_basis: FF-003 analysis (ff003_analysis_20260315T141329Z) confirmed 3 distinct sub-modes across the 6 blocked cases. Per-field accuracy: invoice_date 4/7 = 57.1%, tax_amount 5/7 = 71.4%. First-blocker distribution: verifier (5 cases), arithmetic (1 case). INV-1076 passes because its date (14/02/2026) is unambiguous (14>12) and its tax evidence ('VAT: EUR 0.00') has no embedded percentage.
candidate_interventions: Date-format-aware extraction prompt with explicit ISO output requirement
Tax-line specialist extraction for multi-line tax formats
Phased extraction: extract core fields first, then attempt optional fields separately
Widen date evidence search with format-specific regex (EU dd/mm/yyyy vs US mm/dd/yyyy)
failure_families.invariant_note: 6/7 extended-field records blocked, but INV-1076 passes — not deterministic. Also, sample size (7) is too small for any invariant claim.
failure_families.priority_rationale: 6/20 = 30% of blocked cases. Largest remaining production-relevant family. Two confirmed verifier sub-modes (DATE_AMBIGUOUS, TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH) are potentially addressable by extraction prompt or regex improvements. INV-1074 (arithmetic failure) may warrant reclassification to FF-001/L1.
failure_families.recommended_next_action: Expand gold dataset with more invoice_date + tax_amount test cases; then assess whether (1) requiring ISO dates in extraction prompts resolves DATE_AMBIGUOUS, and (2) a regex improvement for _TAX_ANCHOR_VALUE_RE resolves TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH
failure_families.notes: INV-1076 passes because its date is unambiguous (14>12) and its tax evidence has no embedded percentage. INV-1074 passes the verifier but fails arithmetic (discount line) — possible reclassification to FF-001/L1 overlap.

analysis_result.control_explanation: INV-1076 passes: unambiguous EU date (14>12), no embedded percentage in tax evidence, clean arithmetic
analysis_result.reclassification_candidate: INV-1074 (verifier passes, arithmetic fails — overlaps with FF-001/L1)

failure_families.name: Sparse-tag extraction failure (match_fail only)
failure_families.description: Two invoices tagged only with match_fail fail extraction before reaching the match-determination routing step. These invoices carry no other distinguishing tags, making it difficult to identify the specific extraction problem.
symptoms: Invoice tagged only match_fail, no other descriptive tags
likely_causes: Unknown — these records lack the tag metadata needed to identify the root cause
Possible edge cases in vendor/amount extraction for these specific invoice formats
May be coincidental failures unrelated to the match_fail classification
failure_families.evidence_basis: Both are in the other_blocked sub-cohort. Both are assigned to po_present_match_failed triage cohort. Their tag sets contain only match_fail with no further diagnostic tags.
candidate_interventions: Inspect raw invoice text and extraction attempts for these 2 cases
Add diagnostic tags to characterize failure mode
No rescue policy until root cause is understood
failure_families.invariant_note: N=2, no diagnostic signal. Not actionable as a pattern.
failure_families.priority_rationale: Only 2 cases (8% of blocked). Root cause unknown. Cannot design a targeted rescue without understanding the failure.
failure_families.recommended_next_action: Manual inspection of GLC-4005 and APX-5004 extraction attempts and raw text
failure_families.notes: These may not represent a coherent failure family at all — they could be unrelated individual failures that happen to share a tag.

summary.coverage: 20/20 blocked records assigned to a family (FF-001: 12, FF-003: 6, FF-004: 2). FF-002 (5 cases) resolved by fixture fix.
summary.note: All 20 blocked cases have terminal status EXCEPTION_BAD_EXTRACTION. 5 former FF-002 cases now reach EXCEPTION_NO_PO after fixture fix. FF-003 analysis (2026-03-15) confirmed 3 sub-modes: DATE_AMBIGUOUS (3 cases), TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH (2 cases), ARITH_TOTAL_MISMATCH (1 case). FF-003 is the leading production-relevant target. INV-1074 is a reclassification candidate (verifier passes, arithmetic fails — overlaps with FF-001/L1).
summary.correction_note: FF-001 reclassified from L2 (high) to L1 (low) based on RP-001 prototype findings (2026-03-15). FF-002 reclassified from L2 (high) to eval_fixture_integrity (resolved) based on validation-path trace (2026-03-15): has_po.evidence:null in mock_extractions failed schema validation. Fixture patched, 5 cases unblocked. Post-fix run: run_20260315T133306Z. Priority counts: resolved=1 (FF-002), medium=1 (FF-003), low=2 (FF-001, FF-004).
